description: ASRS Part A clearly elevated (5 of 6 positives), late-identified woman in her late 30s describing classic executive-function struggles. Baseline ADHD-path interpretation.

mustMention: pattern across multiple domains, not just one
expectedQualities.notes: Should engage the executive-function intake content (the gap between intention and action, time blindness, working memory dropouts, capable-but-inconsistent shame), hold diagnostic line, recommend adult-focused ADHD assessment, suggest authors relevant to late-identified women (Hallowell/Ratey, Barkley, Solden), note that overlapping conditions like anxiety/depression/sleep can produce similar symptoms and that part of clinical assessment is sorting that out, close with grounding.

intake.intakeFreeText: I have been wondering about ADHD for a while now. I am good at my job, when I can actually get to it. The problem is the gap between knowing what I need to do and doing it. I will sit down with a clear plan and then it is three hours later and I have reorganized my entire kitchen and the report still is not started. I miss appointments I have written in three places. I lose my keys constantly. I can hyperfocus on something interesting for eight hours straight and then cannot remember to eat. People keep telling me I am capable and lazy in the same sentence and it is exhausting.

screeners.fullName: Adult ADHD Self-Report Scale v1.1, Symptom Checklist Part A
screeners.cutoffMeaning: A score of 4 or above suggests symptoms may be consistent with adult ADHD; the original developers recommend talking with a healthcare provider about an evaluation.
